plan_units.learning_objective: Recognize that core physician traits directly map to innovation leadership traits, reframing innovation as an extension of clinical identity.

plan_units.learning_objective: Understand that intrapreneurship requires balancing clinical vision with organizational environment, resources, and stakeholder alignment.

plan_units.learning_objective: Differentiate intrapreneurship from entrepreneurship and locate the clinician innovator's most immediate arena for impact.

plan_units.rationale: Central conceptual distinction for the module; comparison format should remain intact as a gestalt slide.

plan_units.title: Intrapreneurship vs. Entrepreneurship

plan_units.learning_objective: Analyze why intrapreneurship is harder than entrepreneurship because innovation must occur within load-bearing institutional constraints.

plan_units.rationale: Dense metaphor with multiple explanatory beats and high pedagogical value; benefits from progressive disclosure.

research_goals.pedagogical_intent: Learner needs the primary-source basis and exact visual/formula reference for Sandra Lam's Chapter 4 intrapreneurship framework so the workbook can quote or reproduce it accurately.
Learner needs the original source image or authoritative reconstruction basis for the Idea vs. Opportunity visual from the referenced powerpoint slide so workbook and deck stay aligned.

Learner benefits from validated examples of hospital workflow constraints analogous to legacy IT, compliance, politics, and budgets to enrich application exercises without changing the course's conceptual framing.

depth_delta.deferred_depth: Expanded explanation of each paired step, with brief written examples showing how History maps to Empathize, Diagnosis to Define, and treatment planning to Ideate/Prototype in workflow innovation.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The side-by-side process map and the core analogy.

exercises.answer_key_source_ref: When you take a patient's history, you are performing the first step
exercises.prompt_intent: Have learners explain the mapping between a familiar clinical step and its design-thinking equivalent in their own words.

exercises.answer_key_source_ref: We are just changing the 'patient' from a human being
exercises.prompt_intent: Have learners transfer the mapping to a broken workflow rather than a patient case.

sections.narrative_intent: Provide the fuller explanatory bridge that reassures clinicians they already run an innovation cycle in daily practice.
sections.title: Clinical Method to Design Thinking Crosswalk

depth_delta.deferred_depth: Reading companion notes that unpack the formula's structured logic, especially the organizational realities, resource leverage, and internal alignment components.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The existence of the reading and the central takeaway that intrapreneurship is structured.

exercises.prompt_intent: Prompt learners to summarize how Lam turns a vague idea into a replicable formula.

sections.narrative_intent: Turn the required reading into a guided interpretation tool rather than an isolated assignment.
sections.title: Reading Guide: The Intrapreneurship Formula

depth_delta.deferred_depth: A written constraint-mapping worksheet where learners identify local examples of legacy IT, compliance, politics, and budgets affecting one workflow in their own setting.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The high-level metaphor contrasting blank-canvas entrepreneurship with constrained intrapreneurship.

exercises.prompt_intent: Have learners sort observed institutional barriers into structural constraint categories.

exercises.answer_key_source_ref: redesign the room without breaking the building
exercises.prompt_intent: Ask learners to explain how one constraint changes implementation strategy without changing the underlying goal.

sections.narrative_intent: Deepen the metaphor into a practical diagnostic tool for institutional navigation.
sections.title: Constraint Map for the Intrapreneur's Maze

depth_delta.deferred_depth: A structured template for testing an idea against feasibility, viability, desirability, and execution path in the learner's own organization.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The core distinction that an idea alone has zero value and an opportunity is vetted and actionable.

exercises.prompt_intent: Guide learners to convert one of their own ideas into a preliminary opportunity statement using the course criteria.
Ask learners to judge whether a proposed innovation has a credible path to execution inside their institution.

sections.narrative_intent: Move the crucial idea-opportunity distinction off the slide into a usable decision tool.
sections.title: From Idea to Opportunity Worksheet

depth_delta.deferred_depth: Stepwise deconstruction prompts that break one workflow into assumptions, raw components, and non-negotiable truths before redesign.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The visual three-phase progression from status quo to deconstruction to rebuilt system.

exercises.prompt_intent: Ask learners to identify which parts of a current workflow are inherited analogy rather than fundamental necessity.

exercises.answer_key_source_ref: Building a new system from the ground up
exercises.prompt_intent: Prompt learners to rebuild a workflow from raw blocks into a more efficient design.

sections.narrative_intent: Provide the written scaffolding needed to actually perform first-principles analysis, which would be too dense for the slide deck alone.
sections.title: First Principles Deconstruction Workbook

depth_delta.deferred_depth: Multiple written practice cases using the chief complaint, root cause, and opportunity structure, with room for learner drafting and comparison.
depth_delta.retained_on_slide: The single worked discharge example and the contrast between bad and good framing.

exercises.prompt_intent: Have learners rewrite a solution-first statement into a chief complaint and root-cause formulation.

exercises.answer_key_source_ref: The Opportunity: To design a standardized, mobile-friendly discharge workflow
exercises.prompt_intent: Have learners draft a local opportunity statement for a workflow in their own setting.

sections.narrative_intent: Convert the worked example into repeatable learner practice so the reframing habit transfers beyond the sample case.
sections.title: Problem Framing Practice Lab

lesson_plan.lesson_summary: This lesson reframes clinicians as natural innovators, bridges clinical reasoning to design thinking, introduces intrapreneurship as the primary arena for internal healthcare change, distinguishes ideas from opportunities, teaches first-principles problem framing, and closes with a worked example, synthesis, and knowledge check. Clustered units are used where progressive disclosure materially improves comprehension; comparison, synthesis, and diagram-whole slides are intentionally kept dense as singletons.

plan_units.learning_objective: Understand how first-principles analysis enables construction of a fundamentally better and more efficient system.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From patching inherited workflows by analogy to rebuilding systems from fundamentals through first-principles deconstruction
plan_units.rationale: Resolve beat lands the payoff of the first-principles method.

plan_units.title: The Innovation: Rebuild From the Ground Up

plan_units.learning_objective: Apply rigorous problem framing to reframe a superficial solution idea into a root-cause-based opportunity statement.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From solution-first complaint framing to root-cause opportunity framing through a worked good-versus-bad example
plan_units.rationale: Application block with worked example; multiple beats and strong transfer value justify clustering.

plan_units.title: Worked Example: Good vs. Bad Problem Formulation

plan_units.learning_objective: Recognize why proposing technology before defining the problem is weak problem formulation.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From solution-first complaint framing to root-cause opportunity framing through a worked good-versus-bad example
plan_units.rationale: Tension beat exposes the common error pattern learners are likely to make themselves.

plan_units.title: Bad Start: Solution Jumping

plan_units.learning_objective: Construct a stronger opportunity statement using chief complaint, root cause, and workflow redesign framing.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From solution-first complaint framing to root-cause opportunity framing through a worked good-versus-bad example
plan_units.rationale: Resolve beat demonstrates the target output pattern learners should emulate.

plan_units.title: Good Reframing: Chief Complaint, Root Cause, Opportunity

plan_units.learning_objective: Synthesize the section's core takeaways: clinicians already possess innovation capabilities, intrapreneurship is the primary arena, and opportunities matter more than raw ideas.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From discrete concepts to an integrated clinician-innovator frame through concise synthesis and forward transition
plan_units.rationale: Resolve synthesis slide; should remain dense because its value is the simultaneous recap and transition.
plan_units.title: Part 3 Summary and Transition

plan_units.learning_objective: Verify recall and understanding of idea vs. opportunity, design thinking mapping, Lam's intrapreneurship formula, problem orientation, and root-cause framing.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From exposure to verified comprehension through targeted recall and understanding checks
plan_units.rationale: Assessment is explicitly included in source and is needed for backward alignment to the section's objectives.
source_refs: fall in love with the problem
plan_units.title: Part 3 Knowledge Check

plan_units.learning_objective: Recognize that core physician traits directly map to innovation leadership traits, reframing innovation as an extension of clinical identity.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From seeing innovation as someone else's role to seeing it as a direct extension of physicianship through explicit trait mapping
plan_units.rationale: Core opener for mindset shift; establishes that learners already possess relevant innovation capabilities.
source_refs: You do not need to learn
plan_units.title: Physicianship = Innovation Leadership

plan_units.learning_objective: Understand how the clinical method maps directly onto design thinking steps for operational innovation.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From viewing design thinking as business jargon to recognizing it as familiar clinical reasoning through side-by-side workflow mapping
plan_units.rationale: High pedagogical weight and process complexity, but best kept dense as a side-by-side comparison whose value depends on simultaneous whole-view mapping.
source_refs: you are performing the first step
plan_units.title: The Bridge: Diagnosis = Design Thinking

plan_units.learning_objective: Identify intrapreneurship as a structured, replicable formula rather than a vague personality trait.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From treating intrapreneurship as a vague label to treating it as a structured internal change method through guided reading and formula framing
plan_units.rationale: Prepares learners for the organizational lens that follows and grounds the concept in assigned reading.
source_refs: a calculated approach to driving change from within
plan_units.title: Required Reading: The Intrapreneurship Formula

plan_units.learning_objective: Understand that intrapreneurship requires balancing clinical vision with organizational environment, resources, and stakeholder alignment.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From treating intrapreneurship as a vague label to treating it as a structured internal change method through guided reading and formula framing
plan_units.rationale: Key explanatory support for the reading block; isolates the actionable takeaway learners must retain from Lam's framework.
source_refs: existing resources, brand, and patient population
plan_units.title: Lam Formula: Vision + Organizational Reality

plan_units.learning_objective: Differentiate intrapreneurship from entrepreneurship and locate the clinician innovator's most immediate arena for impact.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From conflating innovation with startup entrepreneurship to understanding internal hospital innovation as intrapreneurship through contrastive definition
plan_units.rationale: Central conceptual distinction for the module; comparison format should remain intact as a gestalt slide.
source_refs: The most immediate impact you can make
plan_units.title: Intrapreneurship vs. Entrepreneurship

plan_units.learning_objective: Analyze why intrapreneurship is harder than entrepreneurship because innovation must occur within load-bearing institutional constraints.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From assuming internal innovation is just entrepreneurship inside a hospital to appreciating the constraint-navigation demands of intrapreneurship through metaphorical contrast
plan_units.rationale: Dense metaphor with multiple explanatory beats and high pedagogical value; benefits from progressive disclosure.
source_refs: without destroying the existing, load-bearing institutional structures
legacy IT systems, compliance rules, and operational budgets
plan_units.title: The Intrapreneur's Maze

plan_units.learning_objective: Identify the relative freedom of entrepreneurship as building from scratch without inherited institutional structures.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From assuming internal innovation is just entrepreneurship inside a hospital to appreciating the constraint-navigation demands of intrapreneurship through metaphorical contrast
plan_units.rationale: First contrast beat; grounds the left side of the metaphor before tension rises on the intrapreneur side.
source_refs: A figure standing in a wide-open, empty green field
building a small house from scratch
Entrepreneur: Building on a blank canvas.
plan_units.title: Entrepreneur: Blank Canvas

plan_units.learning_objective: Understand that intrapreneurs must work around legacy IT, compliance, politics, and budgets to redesign systems safely.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From assuming internal innovation is just entrepreneurship inside a hospital to appreciating the constraint-navigation demands of intrapreneurship through metaphorical contrast
plan_units.rationale: Completes the contrast with the core applied lesson about navigating constraints inside a hospital.
source_refs: inside a highly complex, load-bearing blueprint
redesign the room without breaking the building
plan_units.title: Intrapreneur: Redesign the Room Without Breaking the Building

plan_units.learning_objective: Differentiate a raw idea from an actionable opportunity vetted for feasibility, viability, and desirability within a specific organization.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From casual solution generation to disciplined opportunity engineering through explicit distinction between ideas and vetted organizational opportunities
plan_units.rationale: Highest-stakes concept in the section; central threshold learners must cross before later application.
source_refs: An idea is simply a thought
rigorously vetted for feasibility, viability, and desirability
plan_units.title: Idea vs. Opportunity

plan_units.learning_objective: Recognize that jumping to solutions without validation does not create organizational value.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From casual solution generation to disciplined opportunity engineering through explicit distinction between ideas and vetted organizational opportunities
plan_units.rationale: Necessary tension beat; challenges common learner habits of solution-first thinking.
source_refs: We should use AI to write notes
An idea is simply a thought
plan_units.title: Why Ideas Have Zero Value on Their Own

plan_units.learning_objective: Understand that an opportunity is an idea engineered to be feasible, viable, desirable, and executable in context.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From casual solution generation to disciplined opportunity engineering through explicit distinction between ideas and vetted organizational opportunities
plan_units.rationale: Resolve beat that converts the abstract distinction into the program's standard for opportunity framing.
plan_units.title: Opportunity = Vetted Path to Execution

plan_units.learning_objective: Explain first principles thinking as breaking a broken system down to fundamental truths before rebuilding a better workflow.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From patching inherited workflows by analogy to rebuilding systems from fundamentals through first-principles deconstruction
plan_units.rationale: Conceptually rich three-phase progression with strong visual and pedagogical density; benefits from cluster scaffolding.

build a new solution from scratch
plan_units.title: The Deconstruction: First Principles Thinking

plan_units.learning_objective: Identify the limitation of merely iterating on inherited healthcare workflows.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From patching inherited workflows by analogy to rebuilding systems from fundamentals through first-principles deconstruction
plan_units.rationale: Tension beat surfaces the flawed default mode learners must reject.

plan_units.title: The Status Quo: Reasoning by Analogy

plan_units.learning_objective: Analyze a workflow by decomposing it into its most fundamental truths.
plan_units.narrative_arc: From patching inherited workflows by analogy to rebuilding systems from fundamentals through first-principles deconstruction
plan_units.rationale: Develop beat deepens the method itself by isolating the decomposition step.
